steps.title: Fastest check: compare a table or graph
steps.content: For an equivalent-expression question, enter the original expression and the answer choices in a calculator table or graph. The correct choice must match for several inputs, not just one. The matching choice is C, $v^{3}(v-480)$.

steps.title: Algebra check
steps.content: Use the work below to confirm the identity for every allowed input. This matters because one random matching input is only a quick screen, not a proof that two expressions are always equivalent.

steps.title: Step 1
steps.content: Identify the greatest common factor (GCF) of $v^{4}$ and $480v^{3}$. Both terms share $v^{3}$.

choices.elimination:
• Expand: $(v^{2}+48)(v^{2}-10) = v^{4} - 10v^{2} + 48v^{2} - 480 = v^{4} + 38v^{2} - 480$.
  • This is a degree-4 polynomial with a $v^{2}$ term and a constant, neither of which appears in the original expression.
  • Eliminated because expansion does not equal $v^{4} - 480v^{3}$.

• Expand: $(v^{2}-48)(v^{2}-10) = v^{4} - 10v^{2} - 48v^{2} + 480 = v^{4} - 58v^{2} + 480$.
  • Again this has a $v^{2}$ term and a constant, which the original expression lacks.
  • Eliminated because expansion does not equal $v^{4} - 480v^{3}$.

• Factor out $v^{3}$: $v^{4} - 480v^{3} = v^{3} \cdot v - v^{3} \cdot 480 = v^{3}(v - 480)$.
  • Expanding confirms: $v^{3}(v-480) = v^{4} - 480v^{3}$.
  • That confirms C is correct.

• Expanding: $v^{3}(v+480) = v^{4} + 480v^{3}$.
  • The sign on the second term is positive, but the original has a minus sign: $v^{4} - 480v^{3}$.
  • Eliminated because the sign is wrong.
